rankings.title: IRP1 [4Fe-4S] Cluster Occupancy as Mechanistic Driver of Diurnal IRE-mRNA Control
key_strengths: Closes explicitly acknowledged gap in Nadimpalli 2024
Cheapest and fastest test (native gel, $8K, 2 months)
IRP1 switch textbook biochemistry with quantified predictions
key_weaknesses: Nadimpalli 2024 attributes rhythm to feeding not intrinsic clock
IRP1-C437S chronic iron effects may confound circadian measurements

rankings.title: CISD2/NAF-1 as Circadian ER-Mitochondrial Calcium Timer via [2Fe-2S] Cluster Lability
key_strengths: Triple convergence of circadian inputs (redox, pH, Ca2+) at one protein
Unique MAM position among Fe-S proteins
CISD2 as longevity gene creates aging-circadian clinical connection
key_weaknesses: Cluster half-life (~2h) estimated from in vitro kinetics, not cellular measurement
NADH feedback loop adds 3-4 untested steps
Aging phenotype confound in CISD2 KO mice

rankings.title: Frataxin Iron Donation as Circadian Fe-S Assembly Rheostat Gated by Hepcidin-Driven Labile Iron Oscillation
key_strengths: All cited literature VERIFIED (highest literal groundedness in set)
Lill 2025 Nature stoichiometry finding genuinely new constraint
FA carrier prediction clinical and testable in existing cohorts
key_weaknesses: Ferritin buffering potently dampens LIP oscillation (~<10% LIP from 30-50% plasma)
Mitoferrin may rate-limit independently of LIP changes
FA carrier effect size may be below clinical detection

rankings.title: Peroxiredoxin Oxidation Cycle as Non-Transcriptional Fe-S Clock via H2O2-Mediated Cluster Destruction
key_strengths: Most transformative potential in set if H2O2 gap resolved
BMAL1 KO test is cheapest initial experiment ($10K, 2 months)
HyPer7-mito sensor test directly addresses core quantitative uncertainty
key_weaknesses: Mitochondrial Prx3 resistant to hyperoxidation (Cox 2009 Biochem J)
Matrix H2O2 ~0.15µM, one order of magnitude below >1µM threshold
Edgar 2012 system (RBCs) lacks mitochondria and Fe-S proteins targeted

diversity_check: PASS — all four survivors span distinct bridge mechanisms (redox cluster lability, Ca2+ cascade, iron substrate supply, H2O2 destruction), different Fe-S proteins in different compartments (IRP1 cytoplasmic [4Fe-4S], CISD2 OMM/MAM [2Fe-2S], frataxin-ISCU2 matrix complex, generic labile clusters), and different testability approaches. No convergence detected.
elo_sanity: DISCREPANCY (minor) — Elo ranking: H4 > H2 > H3 > H8. Linear ranking: H4 > H2 > H8 > H3. H3 and H8 swap positions 3-4. The swap captures experiment sequencing efficiency (H3 BMAL1 KO assay cheap/fast vs H8 expensive cohort study) vs groundedness penalty (H3 groundedness 4 vs H8 groundedness 7). Top-2 unaffected. Linear composite is primary output.

H4: Design test separating IRP1 cluster contribution from IRP2 transcriptional contribution
If feeding-driven, what SPECIFIC mechanism connects feeding to IRP1 cluster occupancy?
Predicted magnitude of IRP1 vs IRP2 contribution quantified numerically?
H2: Provide more rigorous CISD2 cluster half-life in cellulo estimate
Does forward direction (clock to CISD2 to Ca2+) stand alone without NADH feedback?
Expected effect size of CISD2 cluster oscillation on mitochondrial Ca2+ uptake?
H8: Published LIP oscillation amplitude in hepatocytes over 24h — is it >10%?
Does mitoferrin expression or activity show circadian variation?
Minimum detectable effect size in a FA carrier cohort study?
H3: Address Prx3 hyperoxidation resistance directly — how do you reconcile?
Would the mechanism work through cytoplasmic Prx1/2 affecting cytoplasmic Fe-S proteins (IRP1, ABCE1)?
Evidence for transient matrix H2O2 pulses exceeding 1µM?
